page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical AMD EPYC 9B14 cores with multithreading enabled. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU accelerators, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks reveal a divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core CPU performance is strong, placing in the top 25% on stress-ng tests, while multi-core performance falls into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, with uncached read and write speeds categorized as poor, measuring 20.8 GB/sec and 11.76 GB/sec respectively. Passmark CPU and memory scores sit in the middle 50%, reflecting average performance for standard integer and cryptographic operations.

Given its high memory density relative to compute resources, the c3d-highmem-4 offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring substantial memory footprints without intensive parallel processing demands. However, the weak multi-core scaling and low uncached memory bandwidth present clear tradeoffs for memory-bound high-performance computing. This instance is best suited for moderate database operations, text processing, single-threaded applications, and lightweight LLM inference using small models.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor and 32.0 GB of RAM. With a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU, it is designed for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local storage or GPU acceleration. Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance, though multi-core scaling and uncached memory bandwidth are weak, placing in the bottom 25% and 10% respectively. This resource profile presents a clear tradeoff: excellent single-threaded execution paired with generous memory, contrasted against limited multi-threaded throughput and memory bandwidth. It is best suited for memory-reliant applications with moderate parallel processing needs, such as text processing, small-scale database operations, and lightweight LLM inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM, offering strong single-core performance alongside moderate multi-core capabilities.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing, strong single-core performance, and a high memory-to-vCPU ratio.

32.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per vCPU
